what_it_does: Declares a module-level AI_TIC dict for 'silent-fallback': silently routing around a problem (switch engine/model, swallow an error, pretend success) instead of failing loud. Carries id, label, markers, and description.
observations: AI_TIC keys: id ('silent_fallback'), label ('silent-fallback'), markers (6 phrases), desc.
markers: "falls back to", "as a fallback", "if that fails, use", "just use a different", "silently", "best-effort".
standouts: Markers capture both error-swallowing ('silently', 'best-effort') and engine-switching ('just use a different') fallback language.
conventions: Same seed-as-data dict shape as sibling ai_tics files.
notes: A seed file defining the 'silent-fallback' tic — flagging fail-quiet / route-around language in favor of failing loud.
questions: Could 'best-effort' as a marker flag legitimate qualified-effort statements?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the silent-fallback seed tic exactly as described.
intention_signals: Encodes Tim's no-silent-failures stance as detectable markers so corpus text describing fallbacks or error-swallowing can be flagged.
novelty: Naming fallback/best-effort phrasing as a catalogued anti-pattern rather than resilient engineering.
contribution: Contributes the 'silent-fallback' tic definition and its fail-quiet/route-around markers to a fused tic catalogue.
summary_for_embedding: A Python seed file defining the 'silent-fallback' AI-tic dict with markers for fallback, error-swallowing, and route-around language.
intention_for_embedding: To detect and subtract silent-fallback behavior, enforcing fail-loud and make-each-thing-work.
